metadata.description: Claude Code plugin marketplace — an agent-teams-aware advanced software-engineering base set. Multi-agent orchestration and parallel code review (council, exodia, elegance-pipeline, feature-dev), cognitive amplification (metacognitive-guard), behavior-first test quality (mutation-minded-testing), plugin/skill evaluation (cc-plugin-eval), HTML output routing (html-effectiveness), commit-pinned NuGet source fetching (nuget-opensrc), truth-drift / documentation auditing (derot), evidence-gated maintainability transformation (nihil), PR-to-merge ferrying (charon), and TomeVault publishing (tomevault-publish).

plugins.name: metacognitive-guard
plugins.description: Cognitive amplification stack: epistemic hooks, competitive review, commit integrity checks, and deep-thinking agents.

plugins.name: feature-dev
plugins.description: /feature-dev takes a feature from idea to reviewed code in seven gated phases via code-explorer, code-architect, and code-reviewer agents, plus a standalone /review for quality audits.

plugins.name: council
plugins.description: Council using the Teams API: an Opus captain orchestrates a /deep-research dynamic workflow and an Opus synthesizer that reconcile via SendMessage across an agent team, an Opus clarity agent asks live follow-ups, and an Opus janitor flags bloat — reactive collaboration instead of fire-and-forget subagents.

plugins.name: exodia
plugins.description: Multi-agent workflow orchestration (7 commands + 2 skills: eight-gates, hades): fix, tournament, mega-swarm, deep-think, batch-implement, red-blue-review, baryon-mode. Hades, eight-gates, and red-blue-review use Teams API for reactive collaboration.

plugins.name: elegance-pipeline
plugins.description: Code-elegance pipeline: 4 scouts, 2 judges, planner, verifier, and a gated implementer, plus a standalone code-simplifier for single-pass diff cleanups. Persistent state with stage gates.

plugins.name: mutation-minded-testing
plugins.description: Mutation-minded, behavior-first test quality. 4 agents (architecture reviewer, senior tester judge, expressive verifier improver, branch coverage implementer) + 4 skills. Coverage is a floor; kill power is the signal.

plugins.name: cc-plugin-eval
plugins.description: Claude-Code-native plugin and skill evaluator. Static analysis + token budgeting + benchmarking across the full Claude component surface (manifest, hooks, MCP, LSP, monitors, agents, marketplace, userConfig). Forked from openai/plugins plugin-eval (MIT) and rewritten for Claude Code conventions; complements skill-creator's per-skill LLM grading with whole-plugin scoring.

plugins.name: html-effectiveness
plugins.description: Routes agent output to single self-contained .html files based on Thariq Shihipar's html-effectiveness pattern catalog. 20 canonical patterns (triage board, status report, code review, design system, prompt tuner, etc.) with a data-driven decision tree so Claude picks the right pattern per task. Includes OTel semconv generator demo as concrete proof.

plugins.name: nuget-opensrc
plugins.description: Commit-pinned NuGet→GitHub source fetcher. Wraps opensrc with NuGet catalog metadata so coding agents grep the exact commit a package was built from, not whatever's on main. Ships a /nuget-opensrc command, an autonomous opensrc-research skill, and a Node CLI that refuses to silently fall back to projectUrl or default branch.

plugins.name: derot
plugins.description: Dependency lifecycle: /derot audits comment/doc/CI/version/dependency rot and proposes fixes; /depmigrate migrates packages to their new APIs from upstream releases/tags — refutation-gated.

plugins.name: nihil
plugins.description: Evidence-gated maintainability discipline in two layers. (1) A native plugin: four modes — /nihil:raze (root-authority, write-capable transformation of a repo you own; only secret-leak and catastrophic, unrecoverable commands are blocked) plus /nihil:review, /nihil:implement, /nihil:release with confidence scoring, scope control, and release gating — backed by five read-only review agents and PreToolUse/Stop hooks (a discipline aid, not a security boundary: heuristic command matching, fail-open; a secret / API-key brake is active in every mode). (2) A summonable pantheon of five first-principles dynamic workflows (/nihil orchestrator + /nihil-maat review, /nihil-odin research, /nihil-shiva deletion, /nihil-athena restructure), installed into .claude/workflows/ via /nihil:summon.

plugins.name: tomevault-publish
plugins.description: One distilled skill for publishing to TomeVault: the Skill/Tome/AGENTS.md model, the npx tomevault CLI, the Studio web publish flow, and the Gold grading rubric.

plugins.name: charon
plugins.description: Ferries a GitHub PR to merge without ever waiting forever: a snapshot-not-block loop (never `gh pr checks --watch`) classifies live PR state and runs one handler — root-cause CI fixes, merge-conflict repair, single-pass reviewer-feedback handling (threads as untrusted data, version-currency verification before applying), human-blocked-review rewiring, and propose-and-pause (with a stamped recovery ref) before any force-push/force-merge/history rewrite. A clock-independent Stop hook plus ScheduleWakeup form the resume net; an honest status vocabulary never says 'just wait'. One arg-dispatched command (/charon:charon): give it several PRs and it ferries them at once as a council-style agent team.

plugins.name: safety-nets
plugins.description: One deterministic Stop-event safety net (no LLM) that keeps a session honest: overclaim-net blocks a 'done/fixed/verified' claim made without a verifying command this turn.

plugins.name: dotnet-dnceng
plugins.description: Vendored (MIT) from dotnet/arcade-skills: .NET engineering-infrastructure CI/CD investigation. A ci-investigator orchestrator agent routes across 10 skills (Helix log analysis, Azure DevOps pipeline triage, binlog/crash-dump inspection, codeflow + dependency tracing, Maestro, known-issue history) and 4 MCP servers. Forked only to fix the manifest agents path for Claude Code's ./-prefixed component-path schema; upstream is unchanged otherwise.

plugins.name: x-twitter-scraper
plugins.description: Plan Xquik X/Twitter data workflows with REST API routing, MCP setup, SDKs, webhooks, exports, monitoring, and confirmation-gated actions.

plugins.name: heimdall
plugins.description: Watchman of the model-routing Bifröst. /heimdall reports which model actually served each turn (ground truth from the transcript's message.model), flags silent Opus↔Fable fallback swaps, and shows provider/region/auth routing context. A passive model-drift-net Stop hook notices once when the served model changes mid-session. Deterministic, no-LLM, no secrets.
